summary: Set 100 Session 2 of 2 (final session of the set): wired the module-row lifecycle actions per the module-lifecycle-simplification verdict. Reworked the module action strip (client.js) and context menu (CustomSessionSetsView.showModuleContextMenu): `Open Plan` survives; the Set 093 authoring actions `AI Plan` / `Import Plan` / `AI Sets` are retired from both surfaces (their underlying flows survive palette-only - dabbler.importPlan, dabbler.generateSessionSetPrompt, dabbler.copyModuleDecompositionPrompt - for legacy repos); `Add Module...` / `Rename Module...` / `Delete Module...` join, DECLARED modules only (the pseudo module keeps only `Open Plan` + `Assign legacy sets to module...` on Unassigned, and gets no lifecycle-management actions). Protocol (`ModuleActionId` in sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts) and moduleActionNarrowing.ts updated to match, with a new declared-only gate for the three lifecycle actions (mirroring the existing pseudo-only gate on assign-legacy). Rename/Delete from the row/context path thread the EXPLICIT-TARGET seam: runRenameModuleFlow / runDeleteModuleFlow gained an `opts.preselectedSlug` that skips pickModule entirely and fails loud on a stale slug (mirroring the Set 093 S2 pickModuleForAuthoring precedent) - the palette commands (dabbler.renameModule / dabbler.deleteModule) keep their own QuickPick unchanged, which is the intended targeting-parity contract, not an inconsistency. `Add` ignores the carried row slug entirely (it always launches the same New Module flow for a brand-new module regardless of which declared module's strip it was clicked from - the row is a convenient place to reach the affordance, not a target). Extended runNewModuleFlow (newModule.ts) to call scaffoldModuleLifecycleSets right after the manifest append - built the just-appended ModuleManifestEntry directly from scaffoldNewModule's known return shape rather than re-reading docs/modules.yaml (a re-read after the async ui.openFile() call would race an external edit and could silently suppress the promised scaffolding); a scaffold-writer refusal now reports in the combined info toast but never undoes the module's declaration (spec, verbatim: 'module without sets beats half-written sets'). New/updated tests: moduleActionNarrowing.test.ts (declared-only gating for add/rename/delete, retired-action drop, dispatch wiring for all five actions), renameModule.test.ts + deleteModule.test.ts (preselectedSlug targeting-parity + stale-slug fail-loud + palette-still-QuickPicks, 3 tests each), moduleAuthoring.test.ts (runNewModuleFlow lifecycle-scaffold happy path with exact 001/002 slug assertions, a renamed cross-module-coexistence test, a real scaffold-refusal-keeps-module-declared test forcing the writer's own mkdirSync collision), moduleLifecycleUi.test.ts (host/client source-scan pinning retired actions absent from both surfaces, new actions present, the declared-only gate regex-pinned on both surfaces, and moduleActionExec's explicit-target binding). Playwright module-tier.spec.ts strip assertions updated: declared strip still 4 buttons (now open-plan/add-module/rename-module/delete-module), pseudo Unassigned strip now 2 buttons (open-plan + assign-legacy, down from 5 - the pseudo module gets zero management actions); the click-focus/tabindex test switched from the retired ai-plan button to open-plan (fire-and-forget, never a blocking modal, unlike the three new dialog-opening actions). Live dogfood on a scratch MULTI-module repo (a second declared module `payments` with its own stamped set, plus an unstamped legacy set, seeded before touching anything) ran the real compiled WRITERS (scaffoldNewModule, scaffoldModuleLifecycleSets, renameModule, deleteModule) end to end: add greeter (plan ready, decomposition blocked on the plan) -> rename greeter->welcomer (zero orphans, both restamped) -> delete welcomer (manifest entry gone, both unstarted scaffolds removed outright per the Set 099 disposition rule) - payments and the unstamped set asserted untouched after every step; raw transcript at s2-dogfood.md, explicitly named as writer-level (not the full interactive command-flow layer, which VS Code's native input boxes and confirm dialogs make impractical to drive headlessly in this harness - that layer is covered by the preselectedSlug unit suites plus ad-hoc UAT Walks 4-6, suggested and non-gating). Suites green: extension unit 1605/1605 (up from S1's 1588, +17 new tests); tsc --noEmit clean; eslint unchanged pre-existing baseline (7 errors in untouched test files); esbuild clean; Playwright Layer 3 full run 26/26 (L-064-12, this session changes Explorer-rendering surfaces); ai_router pytest 3030/6 skipped, unchanged baseline (zero ai_router/ files touched). Cross-provider phased discovery round 1 (fan-out K=2, both gpt-5-6): VERIFIED on both independent calls, zero blocking findings; both calls independently raised the same evidence/precision nits (dogfood wording overclaimed 'multi-module' and 'exactly the moduleActionExec code path'; a test named 'skip-existing' actually tested cross-module coexistence, not same-module re-run; the Add-module numbering test asserted only a digit pattern) plus one real defensive-code nit (the pre-remediation newModule.ts re-read the manifest after an async editor-open call, a race that could silently suppress the scaffold-refusal report) - all five folded in before this disposition was authored: the dogfood script + s2-dogfood.md were rewritten with a genuinely untouched second module and accurate writer-level wording, the misleadingly-named test was renamed to state what it verifies, the numbering test now asserts the exact 001-greeter-plan/002-greeter-decomposition slugs, and newModule.ts now constructs the manifest entry directly instead of re-reading it post-await. Re-verified green (tsc clean, unit suite unchanged at 1605, dogfood re-run PASS); no second verification round was needed since the discovery round was already non-blocking. `docs/spec-md-schema.md` still does not document `module:` / `prerequisites:` (recorded at Set 098 for a Step 9 follow-on, still open, not this session's scope). UAT/E2E suggested: suggestion_disposition 'both' recorded; the existing 6-walk ad-hoc UAT checklist (Session 1's 3 plus this session's 3 new walks covering the strip's retired/added buttons and the Add/Rename/Delete end-to-end click-through) is optional and non-gating. NO version bump, NO publish - single release boundary after Set 101 (module-lifecycle-simplification bundle, operator-confirmed verdict). Set 100 is now COMPLETE.
